title: hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum
intro: Hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum, also known as Scottish hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family that belongs to the genus Hieracium. It is a perennial herb native to Scotland, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to help lower blood pressure due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Scottish hawkweed include its potential to treat kidney stones, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
